_comment: INDEPENDENT validation set. Queries authored by a Sonnet subagent blind to the retrieval ranking code and to the primary probe, to confirm the defines-over-uses lift is not tuned to the primary probe's phrasing. Same 16 ground-truth definer files as real-repo-probe.json. hard=true copied from the primary probe (definer basename shares no concept word with the mechanism).

cases.query: When a fix leaves the suite red, have the agent try flipping comparison and arithmetic operators in the target file automatically and re-run the tests to see if any flip goes fully green.
For off-by-one style bugs, add a fallback that nudges integer literals up or down by 1 or 2 in the target file and reruns the test suite to check if any tweak makes everything pass.
When the model deletes a line before it finishes reading the value it needed, surface a hint telling it to reorder the read before the delete.
The candidate files considered for editing aren't being ranked well — improve how source files get scored so the most relevant one is picked first.
Add a way to recursively crawl the whole repo directory tree and build a map of every file with the symbols it defines.
When the agent stalls with multiple files unlocked for editing, cycle its focus through each file one at a time with a fresh prompt instead of getting stuck staring at just one.
Editing is currently locked to a single file — expand it so the agent can also touch the files that file directly imports, not just the one target.
Before the agent starts working, break the incoming task down into an ordered list of sub-goals so it isn't just improvising step by step.
The agent should be blocked from calling write tools while it's still supposed to be reading and exploring the codebase, only unlocking edits once it moves into a distinct edit phase.
We need automated detection of a metrics regression between two benchmark runs — flag it only when the confidence intervals of the two snapshots don't overlap, not just when the point estimate drops.
The small model sometimes returns nothing but its chain-of-thought reasoning with no actual tool call — detect that case and strip the reasoning text out instead of treating it as a valid response.
Add computation of pass@k along with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals over a set of trial results so we can report eval scores with error bars.
We need a correctness check that just runs the project's test suite and reports back whether every single test passed.
The tokens-per-second monitor is throwing false 'model is degraded' alerts on very large prompts — it should hold off flagging degradation when the prompt size itself explains the slower throughput.
Editing large files as a full rewrite is wasteful — switch to emitting just a single-function patch instead of a whole-file rewrite once the file passes a certain size.
When a search/replace edit block fails to match the file exactly, try a fuzzy re-anchoring pass to find the right location before giving up and failing the edit.
